title: The One With The Late Thanksgiving

jokes.text: Don't you put words in people's mouths. You put turkey in people's mouths.
jokes.setup: Chandler accuses Monica of putting words in people's mouths
jokes.punchline: Monica redirects to her identity as a cook - she puts turkey in mouths
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted character moment that plays on Monica's cooking obsession with good wordplay structure

jokes.text: This is Emma's first Thanksgiving. - No, it's not. - It's not? When was she born?
jokes.setup: Chandler declares Emma's first Thanksgiving dramatically
jokes.punchline: His immediate confusion about when his own daughter was born
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based humor about new parent confusion

jokes.text: What? You are way off, lady. - Am I? Really? Am I?
jokes.setup: Monica defends her cooking skills
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's aggressive repetition escalating the confrontation
jokes.explanation: Functional escalation but fairly predictable aggressive repetition

jokes.text: That's my favorite kind. Okay, we are doing this.
jokes.setup: Discussion of competing with yourself
jokes.punchline: Monica's immediate competitive response and decision
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's competitiveness, well-integrated

jokes.text: Stay out of this, Chandler. This is between me and me.
jokes.setup: Chandler tries to intervene in the cooking discussion
jokes.punchline: Monica treating her competition with herself as a separate person
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic with perfect character integration and quotable delivery

jokes.text: Did you not watch the Dr. Phil I taped for you?
jokes.setup: Monica addressing Chandler about making decisions together
jokes.punchline: Her reference to taping Dr. Phil as relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Monica's controlling nature through pop culture reference

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Happy Meatless Turkey-Murder Day.
jokes.setup: Normal Thanksgiving greeting
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dark vegetarian take on the holiday
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe character moment with her unique worldview and dark humor

jokes.text: Two minutes, twelve pies and a part of one tin.
jokes.setup: Asked if she won the pie-eating contest
jokes.punchline: The specific absurd details of her performance, including eating part of the tin
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect absurd detail that feels authentically Phoebe

jokes.text: This dinner is gonna be so great. In your face, last-year me.
jokes.setup: Monica expressing confidence about dinner
jokes.punchline: Addressing her past self as an opponent
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the self-competition theme with Monica's characteristic intensity

jokes.text: Well, let's see, uh, I know that she has a meeting with her lawyer... and then she has to make a very big poop.
jokes.setup: Ross asking what baby Emma is doing today
jokes.punchline: Rachel treating the baby like an adult with a schedule, ending with bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure though relies somewhat on cheap bathroom humor

jokes.text: Oh, my God. That's the creepiest thing I've ever heard.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggests entering Emma in a beauty pageant
jokes.punchline: Rachel's immediate horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction beat but fairly predictable response to the setup

jokes.text: And compared to Emma, she's a real dog.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining her friend is entering her kid
jokes.punchline: Casually calling another baby ugly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's blunt honesty about appearance

jokes.text: Agh. Phoebe, all babies are beautiful. - Oh, okay.
jokes.setup: Rachel giving the politically correct response about babies
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dismissive, unconvinced reaction
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that shows Phoebe doesn't buy the platitude

jokes.text: And a thousand dollars. ...is something I'm very interested in.
jokes.setup: Rachel condemning baby pageants for just a trophy
jokes.punchline: The money prize immediately changes Rachel's moral stance
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection showing Rachel's quick moral flexibility around money

jokes.text: He still believes that what's on the inside is important.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking not to tell Ross about the pageant
jokes.punchline: Treating Ross's values as naive while she corrupts their daughter
jokes.explanation: Good ironic character moment showing Rachel's hypocrisy

jokes.text: Take the clothes off Joey's Cabbage Patch Kid!
jokes.setup: Need to find cowgirl outfit for baby Emma
jokes.punchline: Solution is stealing clothes from Joey's doll
jokes.explanation: Great absurd solution that reveals Joey's childlike nature perfectly

jokes.text: Did someone drop the baton again? Why come all the way from Kansas to do that?
jokes.setup: Watching what's presumably a parade on TV
jokes.punchline: Joey's confused logic about baton dropping and travel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey confusion showing his simple worldview and flawed logic

jokes.text: I don't get older, I just get better.
jokes.setup: Presumably responding to something on TV
jokes.punchline: Joey's confident self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Fairly generic confident statement, works for Joey but not particularly original

jokes.text: Next year, it's gonna be you, me and little Hemingway Bing.
jokes.setup: Chandler imagining their future family Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: His pretentious choice of baby name
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's intellectual pretensions

jokes.text: What? He's my favorite author. - Name one of his books. The Firm?
jokes.setup: Monica challenges Chandler's claim about Hemingway
jokes.punchline: Chandler names a John Grisham book instead
jokes.explanation: Well-structured reveal of Chandler's pretentiousness being exposed as fake

jokes.text: Oh, Chandler, that's sweet. But you don't have to do everything Dr. Phil tells you to do.
jokes.setup: Chandler offers to help with cooking
jokes.punchline: Monica referencing the Dr. Phil advice from earlier
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that reinforces Monica's controlling nature

jokes.text: I'm serious, let me do something. Just not the turkey. Nothing high-profile.
jokes.setup: Chandler wanting to help with cooking
jokes.punchline: His immediate qualification that he doesn't want anything important
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's self-awareness about his limitations

jokes.text: It's easy to make, and no one really cares about it.
jokes.setup: Monica assigning Chandler the cranberry sauce
jokes.punchline: Her blunt assessment of the dish's importance
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Monica's practical cooking hierarchy

jokes.text: Not with soap!
jokes.setup: Monica tells Chandler to wash cranberries
jokes.punchline: Having to specify not to use dish soap
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup showing both characters' relationship to cooking

jokes.text: You obviously haven't tasted my Palmolive potatoes.
jokes.setup: Monica's warning about soap
jokes.punchline: Chandler's joke about dish soap brand in cooking
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that shows Chandler's quick wit and defensive humor

jokes.text: Three tickets to today's Rangers game! - Dude, I wanted him to guess.
jokes.setup: Joey setting up a guessing game
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately ruining the suspense
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character interaction showing their dynamic

jokes.text: Guess where they are. - Center ice. Did I do something to you?
jokes.setup: Joey trying another guessing game
jokes.punchline: Ross ruins it again, Joey's frustrated response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the guessing game bit with Joey's exasperation

jokes.text: Dude, two times that happened!
jokes.setup: Ross suggesting Joey might get lost at the game
jokes.punchline: Joey's defensive response confirming it happened before
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's childlike defensiveness

jokes.text: Or should I say 'Chan-berries'?
jokes.setup: Chandler talking about making cranberries
jokes.punchline: His pun combining his name with cranberries
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that's fairly forced, though fits Chandler's style

jokes.text: That's some gentle comedy, dude.
jokes.setup: Chandler's weak pun
jokes.punchline: Joey's understated critique of the joke
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary that acknowledges the weak joke while being funny itself

jokes.text: I've been watching this guy over there. I don't think he came with a kid.
jokes.setup: Rachel feeling uncomfortable at the baby pageant
jokes.punchline: Implying a creepy childless man at a baby beauty contest
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about the uncomfortable reality of beauty pageants

jokes.text: I just saw a 1-year-old run around with pantyhose on.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why the pageant is weird
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd image of toddler in adult hosiery
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that emphasizes the absurdity of baby pageants

jokes.text: Oh, I know. We should've been more prepared.
jokes.setup: Rachel's complaint about the weird pantyhose situation
jokes.punchline: Phoebe completely missing the point about it being wrong
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Phoebe's unique moral compass

jokes.text: My God, they just took her sweater off. Look at those arms. Hello, Michelin Man.
jokes.setup: Phoebe assessing the competition
jokes.punchline: Comparing baby arms to the tire mascot
jokes.explanation: Good visual comparison that shows Phoebe's competitive side emerging

jokes.text: Rachel, the hottest babies in the tri-state area are in this room right now.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to convince Rachel to stay
jokes.punchline: Her intense description of baby attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation of Phoebe's investment in the competition

jokes.text: All right, let's give these babies something to cry about.
jokes.setup: Rachel getting competitive about the pageant
jokes.punchline: Playing on the phrase 'give you something to cry about'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Rachel's competitive transformation

jokes.text: No, I'm not letting you put makeup on my baby. - Why not? - Because I already did.
jokes.setup: Rachel refusing to let Phoebe put makeup on Emma
jokes.punchline: Reveal that Rachel already compromised her principles
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect structure showing Rachel's hypocrisy and corruption

jokes.text: Oh! You bum! Get off the ice! - You suck! Get off the ice, you idiot!
jokes.setup: Watching hockey game, getting into it
jokes.punchline: Joey's more intense and crude version of Ross's complaint
jokes.explanation: Basic escalation showing different personalities but fairly predictable

jokes.text: But I gotta say, I am really enjoying nacho chair.
jokes.setup: Ross commenting on Chandler's absence
jokes.punchline: His enjoyment of the food arrangement without Chandler
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Ross's practical enjoyment of the situation

jokes.text: When I was here for 'Holidays on Ice'... I was sitting so far away, Michelle Kwan couldn't read my banner.
jokes.setup: Joey comparing current seats to a previous event
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail about Michelle Kwan not being able to read his banner
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail showing Joey's earnest fan behavior and specific absurd image

jokes.text: They've ruined Cranberry Day.
jokes.setup: Chandler upset about people being late to dinner
jokes.punchline: Elevating his cranberry contribution to holiday status
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Chandler's inflated sense of his contribution

jokes.text: We were at a spelling bee. And I won.
jokes.setup: Need to explain the trophy from baby pageant
jokes.punchline: Rachel's quick lie about adult spelling bee victory
jokes.explanation: Decent improvised lie showing Rachel's quick thinking under pressure

jokes.text: Yes. Y-E-S. Yes.
jokes.setup: Asked about winning spelling bee
jokes.punchline: Rachel spelling out the simplest possible word to prove it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel's commitment to the lie with minimal effort

jokes.text: Grand Supreme Little Darling, New York Division? That's me. Heh.
jokes.setup: Joey reading the trophy inscription
jokes.punchline: Rachel's weak attempt to own the obviously baby-related title
jokes.explanation: Good absurd moment of Rachel trying to maintain an obviously false story

jokes.text: She won a thousand dollars. - So this is an annual thing?
jokes.setup: Phoebe defending the pageant with prize money
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately focusing on making it a recurring event
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's practical side overcoming his principles

jokes.text: That's Alicia May Emory's outfit!
jokes.setup: Joey recognizing Emma's cowgirl outfit
jokes.punchline: Revealing it came from his Cabbage Patch Kid
jokes.explanation: Good callback that pays off the earlier setup and shows Joey's attachment to his doll

jokes.text: And, Rach, while you're in there, throw something on Alicia May.
jokes.setup: Joey concerned about his undressed doll
jokes.punchline: His genuine care for the doll's modesty
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Joey's childlike concern for his doll's dignity

jokes.text: We'll say that we were mugged. You can't get mad at someone who's been mugged.
jokes.setup: Need an excuse for being late
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's logic about mugging victims getting sympathy
jokes.explanation: Good character logic showing Phoebe's unique approach to social situations

jokes.text: Good. But you don't look like you were mugged. No. Here.
jokes.setup: Phoebe noting they don't look mugged
jokes.punchline: Presumably hitting Ross to make him look roughed up
jokes.explanation: Good physical escalation showing Phoebe's commitment to the plan

jokes.text: It looks like Joey has a giant hand.
jokes.setup: Monica looking through peephole at the group outside
jokes.punchline: The distorted view making Joey's foam finger look huge
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that works with the peephole distortion

jokes.text: Oh, they are in for a world of pain.
jokes.setup: Discovering the guys went to the game
jokes.punchline: Monica's dramatic threat
jokes.explanation: Generic threat that fits Monica's character but lacks originality

jokes.text: I got mugged, and they stole my pocket.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his torn shirt with mugging story
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail about thieves stealing just a pocket
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd detail that's both quotable and shows Ross's awkward lying

jokes.text: Or we could plant PCP in the apartment and call the cops on her.
jokes.setup: Brainstorming excuses to avoid Monica's anger
jokes.punchline: Ross's extreme criminal suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation though slightly out of character for Ross

jokes.text: That would be a good way to get rid of all the PCP we have lying around.
jokes.setup: Ross's PCP suggestion
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact response treating drug possession as casual
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd escalation with perfect deadpan delivery from Joey

jokes.text: I hate that thing. It's like a bolt of lightning.
jokes.setup: Reference to Monica's forehead vein popping
jokes.punchline: Ross's dramatic description of the vein
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor that adds to Monica's anger visualization

jokes.text: Ha, ha! I win. - What is that? - That's fire. It beats everything.
jokes.setup: Playing Rock Paper Scissors to decide who goes first
jokes.punchline: Ross making up a new option that conveniently wins
jokes.explanation: Good absurd cheating showing Ross's desperate avoidance of confrontation

jokes.text: Oh, really? Does it beat water balloon?
jokes.setup: Ross's fire beats everything claim
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's equally made-up counter
jokes.explanation: Good escalation matching Ross's absurd game-changing with equally silly counter

jokes.text: Well-played, Phoebe Buffay. Well-played.
jokes.setup: Phoebe beating him at his own cheating game
jokes.punchline: Ross's formal acknowledgment of defeat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's gracious defeat in formal language

jokes.text: When they're having sex on the couch, it's like: 'Come on in, my butt is surprisingly hairy!'
jokes.setup: Commenting on Monica and Chandler usually not locking the door
jokes.punchline: Joey's graphic recollection of walking in on them
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Joey's traumatic experience with oversharing detail

jokes.text: That's a lie! You went to the game. I can see Joey's hand.
jokes.setup: The group trying to lie about taking subway
jokes.punchline: Monica spotting the evidence through the door
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the foam finger visual with Monica's detective skills

jokes.text: For the love of God, take it off!
jokes.setup: Being caught with the foam finger evidence
jokes.punchline: Joey's panicked plea to remove the incriminating item
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation showing Joey's panic at being caught

jokes.text: I had a dream once about a fax machine that did that.
jokes.setup: Monica sliding turkey under the door
jokes.punchline: Chandler's random dream reference
jokes.explanation: Good absurd Chandler moment though somewhat random

jokes.text: How are we gonna decide who gets this? - Water balloon!
jokes.setup: One piece of turkey for multiple people
jokes.punchline: Phoebe suggesting her made-up game solution
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Phoebe's continued investment in her made-up game

jokes.text: Oh, my God, it's Brussels sprouts! That's worse than no food.
jokes.setup: Finding Monica's food in their oven
jokes.punchline: Joey's horror at the vegetable discovery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey reaction showing his food preferences and dramatic personality

jokes.text: Ha, ha! All you got was Monica's stinky Brussels sprouts. Stinky?
jokes.setup: Monica gloating about their Brussels sprouts discovery
jokes.punchline: Her immediate offense at the 'stinky' description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's pride in her cooking overriding her anger

jokes.text: I don't wanna be with them either... but it's Thanksgiving, and we should not want to be together together.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether to reconcile with Monica and Chandler
jokes.punchline: Ross's convoluted logic about togetherness
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Ross's academic approach to family dynamics

jokes.text: Ugh, enough! A monkey could've made them.
jokes.setup: People complimenting his cranberries
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-deprecating response deflating his pride
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Chandler's self-awareness about his minimal contribution

jokes.text: He's doing that weird eye-contact thing. Don't look at him. Don't look at him.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to make sincere eye contact for apology
jokes.punchline: Monica treating his sincerity as manipulation tactic
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Monica's resistance to Ross's manipulation

jokes.text: The floating heads do make a good point.
jokes.setup: Only seeing faces through door chain gap
jokes.punchline: Chandler's description of the visual effect
jokes.explanation: Good visual observation that creates funny mental image

jokes.text: Pies. Oh, we thought you said 'prize.' Here.
jokes.setup: Monica asking about the forgotten pies
jokes.punchline: Rachel's weak excuse and offering the baby pageant trophy
jokes.explanation: Decent attempt at wordplay excuse though fairly weak

jokes.text: It's a day to be thankful. - Don't make me come up there!
jokes.setup: Ross giving Thanksgiving lecture
jokes.punchline: Monica's motherly threat despite being same level
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's maternal authority assertion

jokes.text: That would be a lot more convincing if you weren't drooling. - Ew, is that what that is? - Sorry.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to act indifferent to dinner
jokes.punchline: His physical betrayal by drooling and confusion about it
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Joey's body betraying his attempted indifference

jokes.text: The three of us? - Yeah. You, Chan and the vein.
jokes.setup: Chandler questioning the group count
jokes.punchline: Joey treating Monica's anger vein as a separate person
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that personifies the vein as a character, very quotable

jokes.text: Uh-oh. I'm stuck. - Joey, that is not gonna work. - No, seriously. Unh. I'm really wedged in here.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to squeeze through door chain
jokes.punchline: Actually getting genuinely stuck despite trying to fake it
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation with Joey's plan backfiring realistically

jokes.text: It's a little late for that.
jokes.setup: Joey worried about damaging his head while stuck
jokes.punchline: Ross implying the damage is already done
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan insult showing Ross's occasional mean streak

jokes.text: Can you ever feel your ears? Interesting.
jokes.setup: Joey saying he can't feel his ears while stuck
jokes.punchline: His philosophical realization about ear sensation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey moment showing his unique thought processes even in crisis

jokes.text: Oh, I left them on my bulldozer.
jokes.setup: Asked where his tools are
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic response about his non-existent construction equipment
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm highlighting his white-collar helplessness

jokes.text: I left them at the park.
jokes.setup: Chain of tool borrowing ending with Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual admission of abandoning tools at park
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment showing his irresponsible treatment of borrowed items

jokes.text: I'm finding it really hard not to mess with him. I stuffed Brussels sprouts down his pants.
jokes.setup: Joey stuck helplessly in door
jokes.punchline: Ross and Phoebe taking advantage to mess with him
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback showing characters can't resist messing with helpless Joey

jokes.text: Now, when I get back, I want you and your friends to be gone. Thanksgiving is over. The vein has spoken.
jokes.setup: Monica going to answer phone
jokes.punchline: Treating her vein as the final authority
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that fully commits to the vein personification with dramatic flair

jokes.text: Stop putting things down my pants.
jokes.setup: Continued harassment while Joey's stuck
jokes.punchline: Joey's helpless plea as the pranks escalate
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of the physical harassment but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Look! Look! Look what the... Look what the floating heads did!
jokes.setup: Monica seeing the food mess from Joey getting unstuck
jokes.punchline: Her continued use of 'floating heads' description in anger
jokes.explanation: Good callback though the repetition slightly diminishes impact

jokes.text: I don't care.
jokes.setup: The mess and chaos from Joey's extraction
jokes.punchline: Monica's simple dismissal due to bigger news coming
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing Monica's priority shift

jokes.text: This Thanksgiving kicks last Thanksgiving's ass!
jokes.setup: Getting adoption news on Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Monica's competitive comparison with previous year
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the competition theme with perfect Monica enthusiasm

jokes.text: I'm just so happy you guys are finally getting a kid. I know. Have you considered pageanting?
jokes.setup: Rachel expressing happiness about the adoption
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately suggesting baby pageants for their future child
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Phoebe's obsession continuing and corrupting influence

jokes.text: I went through the exact same thing with Alicia May Emory. The waiting, the wondering. Then one day, I get that call from Toys'R'Us. She was in stock.
jokes.setup: Joey relating to Monica and Chandler's adoption experience
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to waiting for his doll to be available in stores
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that perfectly captures Joey's childlike worldview and creates touching/absurd parallel

jokes.text: That is the exact same thing.
jokes.setup: Joey's comparison of adoption to toy shopping
jokes.punchline: Ross's sarcastic agreement to the ridiculous comparison
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that highlights the absurdity of Joey's comparison
